objective: Observe the smallest bounded protocol-110 multi-channel, multi-record numeric sample sequence needed to falsify or authorize a later runtime-breadth implementation, using independently authored finite IPv4-loopback drivers and the pinned official endpoint only as a black-box oracle.
outputs: A sanitized bounded matrix for double64, int32, int16, and int8 covering exactly two homogeneous channels and three ordered timestamped records in each safely supported direction.

Addressable observations for marker, timestamp, channel-value order, record boundaries, initialization behavior, first failure stage, deadlines, and cleanup without publishing raw bytes or endpoint values.
An evidence-backed decision whether a later implementation may extend the existing fixed-width runtime or first needs a narrower framing correction.
non_scope: Production Rust implementation, public API, feature descriptors or lock, runtime activation changes, String or int64 samples, variable-width framing, empty chunks, multicast, multiple interfaces, non-loopback networking, clock algorithms, queueing, recovery, or broad ecosystem compatibility.

Official implementation source, copied or translated source, new dependencies, unsafe or FFI, devices, private raw captures or machine identities, commands derived from samples, or Manifold authority.

acceptance.proof: Every selected direction and format terminates under explicit discovery, connection, record-count, byte, and deadline bounds with an exact sanitized outcome.

Two-channel value order, three-record order, timestamps, initialization, record boundaries, truncation, extra-record, malformed, cancellation, and cleanup evidence are complete or explicitly classified as unavailable.
Only public APIs and independently authored drivers inform the sanitized observation; raw artifacts remain private and all repository-wide current claims and limitations pass.
Scope, lifecycle, instruction surfaces, public/private boundary, append-only history, and device prohibition remain valid.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the bounded observation, provenance classification, and explicit implementation exclusions.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused observation, public-boundary, and workflow gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: The compact capability router already directs compatibility claims to their owning document and must not expand for observation-only evidence unless validation proves a missing route.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run documentation routing and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Bind exact sanitized observations and limitations.
Bind independently authored driver, configuration, raw-result, and official binary hashes without publishing private payloads.
The policy-owned validation router already defines profile authority; only update if the machine-readable policy gains an explicit LSLC-003O semantic delta.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run policy drift and documentation reconstruction gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Existing LSL ownership and public/private routing already cover observation-only loopback evidence.
Existing evidence, activation, data-plane, and authority guidance already bounds this observation unit.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed installed system-engineering guidance before proposal.

commit_policy: Commit proposal and lifecycle separately; publish only sanitized bounded observations, exact hashes, focused validators, intentional instruction routing, and normal receipts. Preserve every failed attempt and do not commit private drivers, raw captures, endpoint values, diagnostics, caches, or machine paths.
